title: After-Action Report — Substitution Economy Subsystem (Items 1–4)
scope: Four-item spec for O27's continuous 27-out arc: role-capability tagging, 42-45 platoon roster, substitution mechanics and one-way invariant, and manager policy layer driving substitutions

rationale.item: O27 plays nine ironmen with no substitutions; real substitution mechanics match the 27-out continuous structure closer to basketball platoon rotation than MLB

delegation_events.decision: Phase transition: fully manager-timed, no wholesale unit swap
delegation_events.quote: There is no structural 'wholesale unit swap.' The substitution-trigger evaluation scores every potential swap; managers fire when leverage clears a persona-derived threshold.

delegation_events.decision: Special-teams archetype = roster-construction philosophy, not deployment mechanic
delegation_events.quote: Identity expresses in how the FO targets bat-only / glove-only / two-way / specialist counts within the 42–45 band; in-game everyone uses the same situational substitution path.
Existing teams in the DB keep their 34-player shape; the next resetdb produces 42–45.

delegation_events.decision: Role tags: stored at generation on the player dict and DB row
delegation_events.quote: Pure derived attributes (no new talent fields). Re-derived during off-season development the same way archetype is.
Joker insertion still happens as today, but the replaced DH goes to substituted_out and is gone for the rest of the game.
Existing should_pinch_run already had this property; preserved.

delegation_events.decision: Trigger function scaffolded as part of Items 1–3
delegation_events.quote: Routes the new pinch-hit / pinch-run / pinch-field decisions through one seam from day one. Item 4 follow-up tunes the weights and migrates the legacy inline-scored should_* paths.

actions.description: Role-capability tagging: is_hit_capable, is_field_capable_at, field_capable_positions, is_run_capable, is_two_way, classify_roster_slot, encode_field_positions
Roster shape expanded to 42-45 active players with new slot classifications
Database schema extended with role tags and roster slot columns
Player hydration and bench population for substitution candidate selection
Substitution mechanics: Substitution dataclass, Team.bench, substituted_out, is_available, GameState.substitution_log
Trigger function and substitution scoring: score_substitution, substitution_threshold, _log_substitution, one-way enforcement
Test suite for substitution economy mechanics
End-to-end validation script for substitution economy

risk_posture: One-way invariant enforced at every substitution path
Specialist drafting (third pass) guarantees 1 PR + 2 PH per team
Substitution rates verified across manager personality spectrum showing 435× spread
Per-archetype roster tilt applies after snake draft before persistence

validation_claims.claim: 13/13 tests passing in test_substitution_oneway.py including new archetype tilt and threshold band tests
10-game validation: avg 42.0 active players per team, 2.05 subs per team per game, 0 one-way violations
Roster slot mix: bat_first 6.0 / glove_first 8.4 / two_way 6.7 / ph_specialist 2.5 / pr_specialist 1.4 / pitcher 17.0
validation_claims.check_method: Inspect roster generation output from validate_substitution_economy.py

validation_claims.claim: Substitution volume varies by manager personality: 0.03 (dead_ball) to 13.07 (platoon_manager) subs per team per game
validation_claims.check_method: Run 20-game batches at fixed aggression levels per manager personality

non_actions.item: In-place migration for existing 34-player rosters to 42-player shape
non_actions.reason: Deferred. Existing leagues stay at 34 active until reseed. Migration tooling would smooth this transition but isn't required for the mechanic to land.

residual_risks: Roster-shape deviation: actual mix skews glove-heavier (8.4 vs 7 target) and bat-lighter (6 vs 10 target) than recipe
Classifier thresholds favor mid-tier hitters with adequate defense over bat-first placement
Existing 34-player rosters remain unchanged until next resetdb
open_hooks: Classifier tuning: ROLE_HIT_THRESHOLD and _FIELD_THRESHOLDS could be adjusted to pull mix back toward recipe targets
Per-archetype roster tilt could be extended to other archetypes beyond platoon_manager and special_teams
Migration tooling for in-place roster expansion if supporting long-running saves becomes necessary
section_headers_original: Follow-ups (original list — most done in the second pass)
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/manager-policy-aggressiveness-Ah9Bl. Variety report showing 20-game batches at fixed aggression demonstrating substitution rate spread from 0.03 to 13.07 subs per team per game across manager personalities. Second pass (follow-ups) covered Item 4 trigger migration with pinch-hit, pinch-run, defensive-sub, and swap-offensive-for-defense routing through score_substitution. Platoon-edge logic for pinch-hit candidates moved to matchup factor in score_substitution. Classifier tuning raised ROLE_HIT_THRESHOLD from 45 to 50. Per-archetype roster tilt with platoon_manager at 45 active and special_teams at 44 active. Threshold calibration mapped substitution_threshold to [0.55, 0.85] across persona ladder. Explicit specialist drafting helper _make_specialist with new SPEC_PR and SPEC_PH pseudo-positions. DH slot count reduced 10 → 7 to absorb specialist headcount. Existing test suite includes test_declared_seconds (24/24), test_currency (36/36), test_realism_identity (6/6), test_power_redistribute (7/7). Pre-existing failures: test_new_stats, test_template_renders, test_tier_normalization fail on missing flask; test_phase8_db_migration checks hardcoded pitcher_role value removed in Task #65; test_stat_invariants requires pre-seeded DB.
parser_notes: High-quality AAR with comprehensive design decisions, clear architecture description, detailed validation metrics, and follow-ups. Includes two passes of work (foundation + follow-ups) both documented together. Branch reference included. Second-pass updates covering four additional follow-up items integrated into narrative.
